title: testjson + wrapdiag: replace 1MiB Scanner cap with line-reader (oversize → malformed)
description: bufio.Scanner with 1MiB buffer cap is fatal on oversize lines. testjson.ParseStream and scanAsync abort the whole stream — author flagged in code as BUG (pkg/testjson/parser.go:20). wrapdiag.Convert mirrors. Switch to bufio.Reader-based line reader; oversize lines drained and counted as malformed instead of aborting.
acceptance_criteria: ParseStream and Stream continue past >1MiB line; wrapdiag.Convert continues past oversize diag line; both increment malformed counter; tests cover oversize-then-valid sequence.
close_reason: Closed

title: Unify Prior/Diff: PickView gates on r.Diff (drop r.Prior)
description: Post fo-7f5.9 cutover, Report has two delta mechanisms: r.Prior (drives view.PickView's Delta wrapper) and r.Diff (drives JSON contract). attachDiff only sets Diff, so Delta view is unreachable in production. Unify by switching PickView to gate on r.Diff != nil and removing r.Prior. Derive DeltaBucket directions from DiffSummary.New/Resolved/Regressed (severity + prior_severity). State doesn't track tests, so the 'fail' bucket Direction will always be 0 — keep the bucket for layout consistency or drop it (decide at impl time).
close_reason: PickView now gates on r.Diff; r.Prior removed; deltaBuckets derives directions from DiffSummary classification. make check green, smoke-tested 2-run diff still attaches correctly.

title: Regenerate v2 goldens + document deltas before cutover
description:
Parity audit (2026-04-27) found all 33 v1 fixtures drift in v2 across human/llm/json — structural shape changes, not noise. Goldens for v2 were never regenerated. fo-7f5.9 cannot ship the atomic delete until this is resolved.

Scope:
- Regenerate testdata/golden/v2/ for all 11 fixtures × 3 formats (33 renders).
- Write a 'Deltas (v2)' section in testdata/golden/v1/CHANGES.md (or a new v2 CHANGES.md) justifying each intentional change: llm header bump fo:llm:v1→v2, json schema reshape, human banner shape, clean-wrapper UX (the '+ no findings' line).
- Decide json shape for state/diff wire-in: extend Report struct (Option B) vs wrap {report, diff} envelope. Document the call.
- Fix goconst lint in pkg/sarif/builder.go:37 (string 'warning' × 4) — make check is red.
- Update fo-7f5.10 e2e tests to assert against the new v2 goldens.

Out of scope: the atomic delete itself, state+diff wiring, wrap dispatch rewrite — all in fo-7f5.9.

Acceptance:
- make check green
- testdata/golden/v2/ exists with all 33 renders
- CHANGES.md has a Deltas (v2) section, one bullet per intentional drift, justified
- fo-7f5.10 passes against v2 goldens
- json shape decision recorded (in CHANGES.md or bead notes)
notes: Parity audit details: /tmp/fo-parity/results.txt and /tmp/fo-parity/*.diff. Goldens at testdata/golden/v1/{golangci,gotest,jscpd,archlint,gofmt}/.
close_reason: v2 goldens regenerated (33 renders), make check green (goconst fixed via sarif level constants), e2e tests switched to v2 fixtures + new TestE2E_LLMGoldens byte-equivalence guard, deltas documented in testdata/golden/v1/CHANGES.md, JSON shape decision recorded (Option B: extend Report with Diff *DiffSummary)

title: v2 CLI e2e tests: --format human|llm|json against v1 fixtures
description: Backfill the e2e tests dropped in fo-7f5.8 due to subagent timeout. Acceptance: for each of the 11 testdata/golden/v1 fixtures, exercise the new run() dispatch in --format human, --format llm, --format json. Verify exit codes (0=clean, 1=findings) and that llm/json outputs are deterministic (no ANSI, stable ordering). Also add e2e tests for fo wrap {archlint,diag,jscpd} subcommands using their fixtures. Tests should exercise main.run() not internals — they're the contract.
close_reason: Closed

title: Design pass: resolve v2 deferred decisions before implementation
description:
Lock answers to four 'decide in design pass' deferrals scattered across fo-7f5 children. Without this bead they float and get re-litigated at impl time.

Decisions to commit:
1. Paint package name (fo-7f5.2) — pkg/paint? pkg/visual? pkg/ink?
2. Theme count (fo-7f5.3) — one mono+color toggleable, or two distinct themes?
3. pickView heuristic thresholds (fo-7f5.5) — exact numbers for 'top-3 holds ≥50%', Bullet→Grouped cutover (~10 items), Leaderboard trigger.
4. Streaming progressive paint vs drop the footer (fo-7f5.7) — does the renderer have a 'live' mode with footer-redraw, or does live just emit incrementally with no footer at all?
5. (Pulled from fo-7f5.8) LLM as separate format vs sub-style of json — architectural call about audience, not a CLI task. Document rationale.

Output: append decisions to fo-7f5 epic notes; close this bead. Then 7f5.2/3/5/7/8 reference them by anchor instead of re-deciding.
notes: Blocks fo-7f5.2, .3, .5, .7, .8. Cheap bead, prevents drift.
close_reason: decisions locked into epic notes

title: Capture v1 golden fixtures before v2 cutover
description:
Before fo-7f5.9 deletes pkg/render and pkg/mapper, capture v1 outputs from representative real-world inputs into testdata/golden/v1/. Sources: golangci-lint SARIF, go test -json, jscpd JSON, archlint JSON, gofmt diagnostics. For each fixture, record the human, llm, and json renderings as v1 produced them.

These become the diff target for fo-7f5.9 parity verification. Cutover asserts v2 emits byte-equivalent output OR an explicit, reviewed delta (documented per fixture in a CHANGES.md alongside golden files).

Without this, 'parity on representative fixtures' in fo-7f5.9 is vibes. Reviewer flagged 2026-04-26.
notes: 11 fixtures across 5 sources: golangci(3), gotest(3), jscpd(2), archlint(2), gofmt(1). Each rendered 3 ways (human/llm/json) with NO_COLOR=1. Real tool captures where possible; archlint and jscpd-clean synthesized (documented in CHANGES.md). Quirks logged: empty human-format on clean wrapper inputs; nondeterministic generated_at timestamp in llm/json; fo:llm:v1 versioned header. Not committed — staged for dk review.
close_reason: Closed

title: Cutover: delete v1 packages in one commit
description:
Single commit that removes the dying code, after every other child is closed and the new pipeline has parity on the existing fixture suite.

Delete:
- pkg/render/ (entire package; all renderers, themes, tests)
- pkg/wrapper/ plugin layer (Wrapper interface, registry; the three wrap*/ subpackages keep only their Convert functions)
- pkg/stream/ (entire package)
- internal/detect/ (entire package; logic now inline in cmd/fo)
- internal/report/ (entire package; delimiter protocol gone)
- pkg/mapper/ (entire package; substrate has ToReport now)
- pkg/jtbd/ (moves out with the standalone jtbd binary; not part of fo)
- pkg/pattern/{summary,leaderboard,testtable,jtbd,pattern}.go (the dying types; score.go and fingerprint.go already moved in task .1)
- The legacy dispatch in pkg/render/llm.go is dead with the package
- The runJTBD and parseEvents code is dead with main.go's rewrite

Verify post-cutover:
- make check passes
- go vet, golangci-lint, jscpd all still parseable end-to-end
- behavioral output matches design intent on representative fixtures
- bd ready shows no orphaned issues from old paths

This is intentionally an atomic delete commit — no half-state where both worlds coexist.
notes: Atomic cutover landed. Wired Diff via pkg/report.DiffSummary + cmd/fo/state.go (Load → Classify → EnvelopeOf → Append → Save). Added --state-file/--no-state flags; threaded through runStream. fo wrap rewritten to direct Convert calls; deleted pkg/wrapper plugin layer + render/stream/mapper/pattern/internal/detect/internal/report. e2e tests pass --no-state to keep fixtures clean. make check green: 13 pkgs, 50.6%–100% coverage. Smoke verified: --state-file + 2 runs → first run 112 new, second run 112 persistent; --no-state omits diff field.
close_reason: Closed

title: Wrapper functions + new CLI (one entry, --format human|llm|json)
description:
Collapse the wrapper plugin layer and rebuild the CLI.

Wrapper changes:
- Delete the Wrapper interface, registry, blank-import dance, OutputFormat method, RegisterFlags ceremony.
- Keep three exported conversion functions:
  - wraparchlint.Convert(r io.Reader, w io.Writer) error
  - wrapdiag.Convert(r, w, opts) error  // diag still has --tool, --rule, --level, --version
  - wrapjscpd.Convert(r, w) error
- cmd/fo dispatches via a small switch on the wrapper name.

CLI changes:
- One entry: cmd/fo/main.go.
- Two formats: --format human (default for TTY) and --format json (default for piped). LLM as a separate format is gone. If LLM consumption matters as an audience, it's a sub-style of json (terse, deterministic, no ANSI), not a third top-level format. Decide and document.
- Detect logic folded inline (internal/detect package goes away).
- runJTBD path deleted entirely.
- parseEvents (the testjson re-implementation) deleted.
- Theme flag retained: --theme color | mono (or whatever the final theme list is from task .3).

Tests: end-to-end fixtures from real golangci-lint, go test, and gofmt outputs, verifying exit codes and rendered output.
notes: Three formats per locked design (fo-7f5 notes): human (TTY default), llm (piped default, token-dense), json (explicit, machine-parseable). Auto-detect: isatty -> human, piped -> llm.
close_reason: Closed

title: Channel-fed streaming: live mode is the renderer reading a chan
description:
Replace pkg/stream as a separate package. Streaming is no longer a parallel UI bypassing the model — it's a channel feeding the same renderer.

Shape:
- Parser produces a chan ReportEvent (or chan Report).
- Renderer accepts either a fully-materialized Report (batch) or a channel (live).
- Live mode is the renderer reading the channel as the parser fills it; final summary is the same renderer rendering the final Report.

The pkgProgress / footer-redraw / TermWriter machinery from v1 either disappears (if the new renderer can produce live output without a special footer) or moves into the renderer as a 'progressive paint' mode. Decide in design.

This task is the elegant version of the v1 'streaming bypass' fix: one renderer, two arrival modes. No separate pkg/stream, no parallel pattern-less code path.
close_reason: Closed

title: Report type and ToReport methods on substrate (replaces pkg/mapper)
description:
Define the Report struct that flows from parser to pickView to renderer. Single canonical shape, carries everything the visual layer needs: metrics, findings, threshold info, optional prior-run reference for delta, source tool name, run metadata (data_hash, generated_at).

Status enum splits explicitly here: Severity (for findings) and TestOutcome (for tests). No overloaded Status, no compatibility shim.

Add ToReport methods on substrate:
- sarif.ToReport(doc *Document) *Report
- testjson.ToReport(results) *Report

Retire pkg/mapper entirely. Each substrate package owns its own projection to Report.

Tests: golden Report values from fixture inputs (the existing testdata exercising real golangci-lint and go test outputs). Verify Severity/TestOutcome mapping is faithful.
notes: Finding shape carries fix_command (string, optional) — preserves work shipped in fo-ffy/j4o/2kd. Without this, those fields exist in substrate but render nowhere after cutover deletes pkg/render.
close_reason: pkg/report defines canonical shape; sarif.ToReport + testjson.ToReport implemented with golden tests. mapper retirement deferred to fo-7f5.9 cutover (still has v1 callers in cmd/fo).

title: pickView heuristic: Report → ViewSpec selection
description:
Implement pickView(Report) → ViewSpec. The renderer doesn't pick view shape; this function does. Pure, deterministic, table-driven tests for every branch.

Heuristics (refine in design pass):
- count = 0 → Clean
- single PANIC or build-error-only → Headline
- threshold breached → Alert
- top-3 items hold ≥ 50% of total impact → Leaderboard
- count > ~10 → Grouped
- prior run available and meaningful delta → Delta
- per-package/per-module data → SmallMultiples
- otherwise → Bullet

These are first-cut numbers; tune against real fixtures from the substrate. The function is the body of the heuristic — keep it small, readable, and exhaustively tested with table-driven cases per branch.

Depends on: views layer (consumes ViewSpec) and Report type (input shape).
close_reason: Closed

title: Views layer: ViewSpec sum type for the 8 render modes
description:
Build the views layer between data and primitives. ViewSpec is a sum type representing one of eight render modes; each variant carries the data it needs to be painted.

Eight modes:
- Clean — count = 0, single line affirming nothing to report.
- Bullet — small flat list, glyph + label + value per row.
- Grouped — flat list with section labels, used when count > ~10.
- Leaderboard — ranked items with bars; shown when top-3 items hold ≥50% of impact.
- Headline — single huge-by-typography message; used for PANIC, build-error-only, and similar dominant signals.
- Alert — colored bold prefix + value; used when a metric breaches threshold.
- Delta — value + arrow + magnitude vs prior run; shown when a comparison is available.
- SmallMultiples — Swiss grid of repeated Bullet/Bar units, aligned by whitespace columns. Used for per-package or per-module summaries.

Each variant is a struct, not an interface dispatch. The renderer is a thin paint over ViewSpec; no logic in render time beyond layout. Card and Dashboard collapse into Section (a labeled block of any of the above) — no border or chrome.

Tests: each view variant rendered with sample data; golden output strings; both color and mono mode covered.
notes: pkg/view landed: 8 ViewSpec variants (Clean/Bullet/Grouped/Leaderboard/Headline/Alert/Delta/SmallMultiples) under closed-marker interface; Render(spec, theme, width) type switch; mono goldens + escape-presence color smoke tests for severity/outcome/delta-arrows; FixCommand surfaced under Bullet/Grouped rows. 17/17 tests pass; lint clean for view pkg (2 pre-existing issues in sarif/main.go remain).
close_reason: Closed

title: Theme system: mono-first, color layered on top, NO_COLOR fallback
description:
Design the theme that primitives and views render through. Mono-first: typography hierarchy (bold, dim, uppercase) carries structure when color is unavailable. Color layers on top semantically — red=bad, green=good, yellow=warning, dim=context.

In scope:
- One color theme designed for both light and dark terminals (or two if needed — decide in design pass). No 'orca' carry-over from v1; it's deleted unless explicitly wanted on aesthetic grounds.
- Mono theme as the design baseline. Output must read in mono before color is added.
- NO_COLOR env var honored.
- Severity → color mapping (Severity enum, not the old Status).
- Goodness mapping for delta/trend coloring (up-is-good vs up-is-bad direction encoded per metric).

Tests: sample renderings in both color and mono mode. Deterministic ANSI sequences for golden tests. Verify mono output is fully usable without color.
close_reason: pkg/theme implemented: mono base, color overlay, NO_COLOR + non-TTY both force mono. Glyph set covers all v2 views (severity, outcome, bar, delta).

title: Paint primitives: bar, sparkline, glyphs, alignment (no box-drawing)
description:
Build the visual primitive library that views render through. Tufte-Swiss aesthetic: every mark earns its keep, hierarchy from data and alignment, not chrome.

In scope:
- Horizontal bar with optional threshold marker. Width-budgeted (caller passes max width). Color via theme. Threshold position computed from value range.
- Sparkline / trend strip: ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█ cells, mean-within-bucket bucketing, fixed cell count from caller.
- Level glyphs (single-cell indicators) and direction arrows (↑ ↓ →) with goodness-keyed coloring.
- Alignment helpers: padRight/padLeft (already exist; harden with rune-width awareness for unicode), column alignment with truncation, multi-column row builder.
- Width budgeting: terminal-width detector, gracefully-degrading layout when width < threshold.

Out of scope (do NOT build):
- Box-drawing (┌ └ ─ │). No card frames, no dashboard chrome, no separator rules.
- Theme palettes (separate task).

Package: new pkg/paint (or similar — name in design pass). Pure functions where possible. Tests: deterministic output strings, golden-file style for primitives that compose.
close_reason: pkg/paint: Bar, Sparkline, PadLeft/Right, Columnize. Pure functions, glyphs passed in. Tested edge cases (NaN, zero, ragged rows).

title: Pin and audit substrate (sarif, testjson, score, fingerprint)
description:
Verify the keep-list packages have no upstream imports back into the dying code:
- pkg/sarif (reader, types, builder, aggregates)
- pkg/testjson (parser, stream, types, stats, funcresults)
- pkg/pattern/score.go
- pkg/pattern/fingerprint.go

Score and Fingerprint currently live inside pkg/pattern alongside types that will be deleted. Move them to a permanent home (proposal: pkg/score, pkg/fingerprint — single-file packages). Verify their tests still pass.

Run grep audits to confirm no substrate file imports pkg/render, pkg/mapper, pkg/wrapper, pkg/stream, internal/detect, internal/report, pkg/jtbd, or any pattern type that's being deleted. If found, simplify or excise the offending import before tagging.

Deliverable: tagged commit on a branch where every substrate package's tests pass and no substrate file references doomed code. After this, no later phase modifies substrate.
notes: Tag the pinned-substrate SHA as 'v2-substrate-pinned' so future audits and the cutover commit can reference it unambiguously.
close_reason: substrate pinned: pkg/{sarif,testjson,score,fingerprint} clean of doomed-package imports. Tag v2-substrate-pinned at 39b243e on branch v2-substrate-pin.

title: fo v2: redesign render pipeline around Tufte-Swiss visual model
description:
Rebuild fo's render pipeline as a content-aware Tufte-Swiss visualizer for terminal output. No backward compatibility; legacy code is deleted at cutover. JTBD is out of scope — it becomes a separate utility that emits a canonical format fo can consume.

Substrate kept (frozen during the rebuild): pkg/sarif, pkg/testjson, plus pattern.Score and pattern.Fingerprint extracted to permanent home.

Replaced: the entire visual layer (paint primitives, views, pickView, theme), the pipeline (Report type, ToReport methods on substrate, channel-fed streaming), the CLI (one entry, two formats: human for TTY, json for piped).

Deleted at cutover: pkg/render, pkg/wrapper plugin layer (collapses to three exported Convert(r,w) functions), pkg/stream as a separate package, internal/detect, internal/report (delimiter protocol dies — no multi-tool multiplexing), pkg/mapper, pkg/jtbd (moves out with the standalone jtbd binary), the legacy llm.go dispatch, the LLM mode as a separate format, pattern.Summary/Leaderboard/TestTable/Error/JTBDCoverage. Status enum splits into Severity and TestOutcome with no compat layer.

Aesthetic: Tufte-Swiss. No box-drawing, no card frames, no dashboard chrome. Hierarchy comes from color, type weight, alignment, and whitespace. Mono theme designed first; color theme layers on top. Eight view modes: Clean, Bullet, Grouped, Leaderboard, Headline, Alert, Delta, SmallMultiples — selected by pickView from the Report's shape.

Audit context (so future sessions don't re-derive): in v1, OutputFormat() returns one value (FormatSARIF) and is never called from production code; RegisterFlags is no-op in archlint and jscpd; pkg/stream bypasses Pattern entirely; llm.go runs both a new direct dispatch and a legacy SummaryKind dispatch on every call; main.go is 460 lines with three command paths and a parseEvents that re-implements testjson NDJSON parsing.
notes:
## v2 Design Decisions (locked 2026-04-26 via fo-4dv)

1. **Paint package**: pkg/paint
2. **Theme**: one Theme interface, two presets (mono.go, color.go). NO_COLOR forces mono regardless of TTY.
3. **pickView thresholds**:
   - Clean: count == 0
   - Headline: PANIC | build-error-only | single dominant severe signal
   - Alert: any single metric breaches threshold
   - Leaderboard: total >= 6 AND top-3 hold >= 50% of impact
   - SmallMultiples: groups >= 3 AND each group >= 2 items
   - Grouped: count > 10 (and none of above)
   - Delta: prior-run present AND any non-zero bucket (composable wrapper over another view)
   - Default: Bullet
4. **Streaming**: drop the footer. Live mode emits incrementally as parser yields; final summary on channel close. No terminal-state machinery, no progressive paint mode.
5. **Formats: three** — human (TTY, Tufte-Swiss), llm (piped default, token-dense plaintext), json (explicit, machine-parseable). Auto-detect: TTY -> human, piped -> llm. Drop v1's dual-dispatch in llm.go; one clean LLM renderer.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: fix_command generator: testjson (failed test → go test -run command)
description: Generate FixCommand for failed tests in pkg/testjson stream parser: 'go test -run ^TestFoo$ ./pkg/bar -v'. Different code path from SARIF wrappers. Requires fo-j4o (Finding shape). Source: gh #245.
close_reason: FixCommand populated for failed tests (subtest regex ^Test$/^sub$), panics (pkg-level go test), build errors (go build). Tests added in pkg/mapper/testjson_fixcmd_test.go. make check clean.

title: fix_command generators: per-wrapper (wrapdiag/wrapjscpd/wraparchlint)
description: Implement FixCommand generation in each wrapper during SARIF construction. wrapdiag → tool-aware (e.g. golangci-lint → 'golangci-lint run --fix --enable-only=<rule> <file>'). wrapjscpd → grep-ready file paths + line ranges. wraparchlint → 'go-arch-lint check'. Requires fo-j4o (Finding shape). Source: gh #245.
close_reason: per-wrapper FixCommand generators shipped (wrapdiag tool-aware, wrapjscpd grep-hint, wraparchlint check-rerun); SARIF Fix carrier added; mapper plumbed; tests cover populated + omitted cases

title: fix_command: extend Finding + render plumbing (llm/json)
description: Add FixCommand string field to pkg/pattern Finding. Wire through llm + json renderers (mechanical pass-through). NO generator logic in this bead — that's split into sibling tasks per source. Establishes the field shape so other beads can extend Finding without merge conflicts. Source: gh #245.
close_reason: Added FixCommand to TestTableItem, wired through llm (fenced bash block, skip-empty) and json (pass-through) renderers; 4 new tests

title: State + diff engine: I/O, classify, render, flake/regression, CLI
description: State + diff engine for fo-40z. Sidecar .fo/last-run.json (configurable via --state-file) with HISTORY SLOTS in schema (≥2 prior runs) so flake detection doesn't force later rewrite. Atomic write. Tolerate missing file (first run). Diff classifier: new (current only), persistent (both, severity unchanged), resolved (prior only), regressed (both, severity ↑), flaky (resolved-then-new across ≥2 runs). Render: human+llm headline band ('3 new · 2 resolved · 1 regressed · 2 flaky · 41 persistent'); llm/json envelope diff block (new[],resolved[],regressed[],flaky[],persistent_count). Flags: --no-state, --state-file, fo state reset subcommand. Part of epic fo-40z (gh #248).
notes: v2 collision (per review 2026-04-26): build natively against v2 substrate. Delta ViewSpec lives in fo-7f5.4, headline band consumes Report (fo-7f5.6). Do NOT build against pkg/render — deleted at fo-7f5.9 cutover. Per locked design (fo-7f5 notes): three formats — emit headline+envelope in both llm and json variants.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stable finding fingerprint: sha256(rule_id+file+normalized_message)
description: Compute stable fingerprint per finding: sha256(rule_id + file + normalized_message). Excludes line number so unrelated edits don't invalidate identity. Add Fingerprint string to pkg/pattern Finding. Mapper populates during SARIF→pattern. INCLUDES golden-file stability tests across (a) line shift, (b) message normalization (paths, line numbers in message text, addresses), (c) rule_id rename. Required for diff classifier and #249. Part of epic fo-40z (gh #248).
close_reason: Stable fingerprint helper (sha256 of rule_id+file+normalized_message) added in pkg/pattern/fingerprint.go; populated by SARIF and testjson mappers; 14 stability tests covering line shift, path prefix, rule_id rename, normalization edge cases.

title: Temporal awareness: diff fo runs against last-run state
description: Give fo memory of the previous run so every report narrates what changed, not just what exists. Sidecar .fo/last-run.json keyed by stable fingerprint sha256(rule_id + file + normalized_message). Classify findings as new/persistent/resolved/regressed/flaky. Emit headline band + diff envelope. Source: gh #248. The 10x upgrade for fo's primary consumers (snipe, trixi, magloop).
close_reason: all steps complete

title: QA: invariants + pipeline goldens for pkg/view
description: Add table-driven invariant tests over PickView (no two leaderboard rows share a label, total == sum of row values, deterministic, etc.) and golden fixtures captured from real tool pipelines (jscpd-only, vet-only, mixed, clean, panic, build-error). Layer 1 prevents picker logic regressions; Layer 2 prevents end-to-end format regressions. Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-27-view-invariants-and-goldens.md
close_reason: invariants_test.go + pipeline_golden_test.go landed; covered in plan 2026-04-27-view-invariants-and-goldens.md

title: cmd/fo: replace io.ReadAll(stdin) with bounded/streaming consume
description: Audit 2026-04-27 (PASS 1 #1, PASS 2 #2): both run() and runStream() buffer the entire stdin via io.ReadAll before rendering. Large CI runs with verbose go test JSON or SARIF can OOM the process and also delay Ctrl-C cancellation (cancel only fires after the read returns). Stream mode is supposed to stream — currently doesn't. Fix: incremental parse/render pipeline that periodically checks ctx.Done(); add integration test with a generated large NDJSON asserting bounded RSS and prompt cancel. Source: docs/code-reviews/true-bug-audit-2026-04-27.md
close_reason: Closed

title: leaderboard view: aggregate rows by label, drop single-label case
description: pkg/view/pickview.go pickLeaderboard creates one LbRow per Finding with Label=RuleID. When N findings share a ruleId (e.g. 6 jscpd code-clones), the renderer emits N identical-looking rows instead of one aggregated row. Repro: pipe a jscpd-only SARIF stream with 6 findings to fo --format human; see 6 lines of 'code-clone  ###  2'. Fix: aggregate rows map[label]float64 summing scores, then sort; if len(rows)==1 after aggregation, return false so view falls through to bullet/grouped.
close_reason: aggregate by label + single-label fall-through; tests in pkg/view/invariants_test.go

title: cmd/fo runStream: write errors to injected stderr, not os.Stderr
description: runStream takes stderr io.Writer parameter then ignores it (cmd/fo/main.go:370/375/385) — writes to os.Stderr directly. Breaks library/test embedding.
close_reason: Closed

title: Migrate sandbox layout from .codex/ to .sandbox/
description: Codex cloud agent looks for .sandbox/codex/setup.sh (matching trixi's go-sandbox v0.2.0 layout). fo still uses legacy .codex/. Move .codex/→.sandbox/codex/, .bin/→.sandbox/bin/, lib-doctor.sh→.sandbox/lib/, update Makefile/AGENTS.md/.gitignore.
close_reason: Closed

title: report multiplexer protocol missing — '--- tool:X format:Y ---' streams not parsed
description:
CLAUDE.md:34 documents 'internal/report/ — Report delimiter protocol (multiplexer for multi-tool pipelines)' but the package no longer exists in the v2 codebase. fo emits 'unrecognized input (expected SARIF or go test -json)' when fed a multi-section stream with '--- tool:vet format:sarif ---' style delimiters.

Impact: trixi's make report and snipe's make report (both pipe a multi-tool stream into 'fo --format llm') silently fail — the '|| true' masks it. npharvester now uses lint-only as a workaround.

Decide: restore the multiplexer, or remove the protocol from CLAUDE.md and update consumers (trixi/snipe) to either lint-only or sequential fo invocations.
close_reason: restored multiplexer in pkg/report/multiplex.go; wired into parseToReport with per-section parse-error synthesized findings; verified via trixi make report-human

title: Add data_hash + generated_at to report envelope
description: Add stable envelope to fo --format llm / json: data_hash (sha256 short of concatenated input stream) + generated_at (RFC3339). Computed once during input read in cmd/fo/main.go. Header block in pkg/render/llm.go + pkg/render/json.go. Human renderer unchanged. Enables agent-side caching, magloop idempotency, paste-in bug-report matching. Source: gh #244.
close_reason: Added data_hash (sha256[:12]) + generated_at (RFC3339) to llm/json envelopes. Hash computed once in cmd/fo after stdin read; threaded via render.RunMeta + WithMeta. Human renderer unchanged. 4 new tests, make check clean.

title: Per-tool status field in multi-tool report stream
description: internal/report delimiter protocol gains optional status:X attribute (ok|clean|partial|timeout|skipped|error). Wrappers report own status; multiplexer reports missing-tool/timeout. Renderers surface as badge per tool section. Fixes silent-failure ambiguity (empty slice today reads as clean even on segfault). Source: gh #246.
close_reason: Implemented per-tool status:X attribute on report delimiter protocol with backward-compat default derivation (whitespace→clean, parse error→error, else→ok). Wrappers untouched (delimiters are caller-emitted). Renderers: llm always emits per-tool header with [status: X, value]; human emits [status] badge for non-default statuses; json field round-trips. 9 new tests + 3 updated invariant tests. make check clean.

title: Deterministic priority score per finding
description: Compute score = severity_weight × occurrence_count × file_centrality. severity: error=3, warning=2, note=1. centrality: 1.0 for cmd/+pkg/ roots, 0.5 internal/, 0.25 _test.go. Add Score float64 to pkg/pattern Finding; mapper computes from SARIF; renderers sort by score desc. Tunable weights as constants. Source: gh #247.
close_reason: Score helper, mapper integration, renderer sort, tests, make check clean.

title: report.ParseSections: warn on non-empty prelude before first delimiter
description: Audit 2026-04-27 (PASS 3 #3): ParseSections silently discards lines before the first delimiter. If an upstream wrapper accidentally emits a banner/prelude (or sections are mis-ordered), the first tool's output is lost with no error. Fix: when non-whitespace content precedes the first delimiter, return a parse warning/error (or surface a synthetic finding). Test: prelude + valid sections produces explicit warning. Source: docs/code-reviews/true-bug-audit-2026-04-27.md
close_reason: Implemented + merged to main; verified by full test suite

title: wrapdiag: surface oversize-line drops instead of silently discarding
description: Audit 2026-04-27 (PASS 3 #2): diag.readAndAdd reads (line, oversize, err) but ignores the oversize bool and just calls addLine — extremely long diagnostic lines are dropped silently. A critical lint finding with a huge message/path can vanish, yielding a false-clean SARIF. Fix: count oversize drops; emit a stderr warning or synthetic SARIF note. Test: feed an oversize line and assert the marker. Source: docs/code-reviews/true-bug-audit-2026-04-27.md
close_reason: Implemented + merged to main; verified by full test suite

title: parseToReport: fall back to tolerant testjson parser when first byte isn't '{'
description: Audit 2026-04-27 (PASS 1 #2): non-stream parseToReport hard-rejects input whose first non-space byte isn't '{', but testjson.ParseStream itself tolerates malformed lines. Result: any prelude/banner from a wrapping script before valid go test JSON makes fo exit usage error instead of rendering. Fix: if sniffing fails, attempt testjson.ParseBytes and accept when ≥1 valid event parsed. Test with prelude + valid JSON. Source: docs/code-reviews/true-bug-audit-2026-04-27.md
close_reason: Tolerant testjson fallback added; covered by TestParseToReport_TolerantTestJSONPrelude

title: wrappers: bound io.ReadAll to prevent OOM on pathological tool output
description: cmd/fo/main.go run() and wrappers wrapjscpd/wraparchlint use io.ReadAll on stdin. Pathological tool output can exhaust memory. Wrap reads with io.LimitReader (configurable cap, e.g. 256MiB default) and emit a graceful 'input too large' error.
close_reason: wraparchlint/wrapjscpd now use boundread.All (256MiB cap, ErrInputTooLarge)

title: testjson.Stream: refuse non-Closer readers in cancellable mode (or wrap)
description: If r is not io.Closer, ctx cancel cannot unblock the scanner goroutine — leaks. Currently documented but unenforced. Either tighten signature to io.ReadCloser, or wrap non-closers in an internal pipe.
close_reason: Signature tightened; cancel verified by TestStream_CancelClosesReader

title: Dedup pkg/sarif/builder.go WithXxx methods
description: jscpd flagged two nearly identical builder methods at pkg/sarif/builder.go L45-69 and L76-100. Real duplication in production code (not test fixture parity). Low priority — surface when that file is next touched. Candidate fix: extract a shared helper that takes the differing field as a parameter, or generate via small reflection-free pattern. Keep changes minimal; don't over-abstract.
close_reason: Inlined dedup; AddResult now delegates to AddResultWithFix

title: Decision: should fo auto-create beads from findings?
description: DECISION bead, not implementation. Weigh: noise control (gate on severity ≥ error? only new/regressed from #248 diff? per-wrapper opt-in?); --beads flag shape (writes to .beads or emits JSONL on side channel?); fingerprint reuse (same hash as #248 enables update-not-duplicate); dependency wiring (link beads from same file/rule for graph views); user fit (issue-runner + fixnext skills). Output: a written decision (recommend/decline + rationale + scope if recommended) appended to issue #249. Blocked by fo-40z (#248) and the fix_command bead (#245). Source: gh #249.

title: state.Save: fsync parent dir after rename for crash-consistency
description: .fo/last-run.json save does fsync(tempfile) + rename, but no fsync(parent dir). On crash/power-loss after rename, metadata may not be durable on some filesystems.
close_reason: Implemented + merged to main; verified by full test suite

value: When closing an epic, close its open children in the same pass — don't leave orphans. Check deps for siblings the epic's work may have resolved.

fo v2 epic (fo-7f5) review applied 2026-04-26: wired missing deps (4→6, 2→3, 7→4, 7→5), filed fo-lxf (parity fixtures, blocks 7f5.9), filed fo-4dv (design pass, blocks 2/3/5/7/8), rebound fo-40z.2 onto v2 substrate (deps on 7f5.6+7f5.4 + collision note), bumped 7f5.9 to $8, added rollback/soak notes to 7f5.9, fix_command notes to 7f5.6/7f5.4, substrate-tag name 'v2-substrate-pinned' on 7f5.1. Skipped: bulk acceptance-criteria sweep (mechanical, separate pass), epic est_cost_usd roll-up, fo-4un re-read.

Epic/singleton from a GH issue → set gh_issue=<N> metadata at create (number only, no #). Children inherit via parent; don't duplicate. Lookup: bd query -a --json | jq '.[]|select(.metadata.gh_issue=="N")'. On close: include 'Closes #N' in commit or gh issue close N.
